reflexion_entries_to_archive.lesson: Both CHECK seats filed CRITs on the D4 diff; the only real one (post-sign yield window) was found by checking WHERE the await yields, while two others died on exact arithmetic (a rival fires at the SAME epoch, not higher - routing to a different, safe code path) and one was fabricated (invented setTimeout(grace+null) when the code has an explicit null gate). Triage = re-derive the failing interleaving's numbers yourself against the shipped lines; severity labels from external reviewers carry zero evidential weight.

The S123 handoff and BACKLOG both listed B2(c) as open work worth a session slot; a 2-minute A.0 grep found it shipped 4 sessions earlier (S120 P3, 3fc6688) with its own lock test. Roadmap prose drifts; git+code never do. Every priority candidate gets a state probe before it enters a PDR - this is the second instance (S114 G3b), which makes it a pattern, not an accident.

A heap delta alone cannot distinguish a render leak from organic world growth (D+3.08MB looked ambiguous until the entity counts explained it). The display-object census made the verdict decisive for free: renderer leaks show as census growth DECOUPLED from entity counts, which is a structural signal immune to GC noise. When auditing a subsystem for leaks, instrument the subsystem's OWN object population, not just bytes.
